taskDescription: Arrange the branches of the lumbar plexus from superior (L1) to inferior (L4) by their root values (AN45.2).
paragraphs: Iliohypogastric nerve (L1) — supplies the skin over the hypogastric region and lateral gluteal area
Ilioinguinal nerve (L1) — traverses the inguinal canal, supplies skin of groin and scrotum/labium majus
Genitofemoral nerve (L1, L2) — genital branch supplies cremaster; femoral branch supplies skin over femoral triangle
Lateral femoral cutaneous nerve (L2, L3) — pure sensory; compression causes meralgia paraesthetica
Femoral nerve (L2, L3, L4) — largest branch; supplies quadriceps femoris and skin of anterior thigh
Obturator nerve (L2, L3, L4) — supplies adductor muscles and skin of medial thigh
